mechanism_narrative: A persistently low and stable FEDFUNDS rate (near 1.15% as of Sep-2017) signals continued accommodative policy that keeps discount rates low, supporting equity valuations and producing positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days as long as the level stays below its 12-month trailing average.
evidence_refs: fact:FEDFUNDS at 1.15% Sep-2017, below prior tightening-cycle peaks
failure_condition: Rank IC between FEDFUNDS level and 20-day forward SPX returns falls below 0.03 over any rolling 2-year window, or positive-return hit rate in low-rate periods is statistically indistinguishable from the unconditional 65.5% base rate.

mechanism_narrative: A rise in UNRATE of more than 0.3 percentage points over a rolling 3-month window signals an inflection toward labor-market deterioration that historically precedes broader economic slowdown, producing negative S&P 500 forward returns over the following 60 trading days.
regime_hint: late-cycle expansion with tightening labor market
failure_condition: In quarters where the 0.3pp UNRATE trigger fires, 60-day forward SPX returns are non-negative in more than 55% of instances, or the signal's Rank IC is not significantly different from zero (p > 0.10) over a 3-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous acceleration in RSAFS (retail sales growth) and HOUST (housing starts growth) reflects broad-based consumer and housing demand strength that jointly signals robust near-term aggregate demand, producing positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days; neither series alone carries comparable predictive power.
candidate_trigger: RSAFS 3-month annualized growth > 3% AND HOUST 3-month annualized growth > 5%
evidence_refs: fact:RSAFS Sep-2017 vintage 483,895M; HOUST Aug-2017 vintage 1,180K units
regime_hint: expansion phase with synchronized consumer and housing strength
failure_condition: The joint-trigger sample shows forward 20-day SPX return distribution statistically indistinguishable from the unconditional distribution (Kolmogorov-Smirnov p > 0.10), or interaction term adds no incremental Rank IC versus either series alone.

mechanism_narrative: During late-cycle expansions, decelerating INDPRO growth (industrial production momentum fading) signals approaching capacity constraints and margin compression that precede equity market underperformance, producing negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 60 trading days; this relationship is muted or reversed in early-cycle recoveries.
evidence_refs: fact:INDPRO Aug-2017 vintage 104.739 vs revised 99.6925, indicating decelerating momentum after revision
failure_condition: In late-cycle windows (identified by UNRATE below 5% and prior 24-month positive PAYEMS growth), INDPRO deceleration fails to precede negative 60-day forward returns in more than 55% of episodes, or Rank IC < 0.03 over any 2-year rolling late-cycle window.

mechanism_narrative: Positive month-over-month PAYEMS growth reflects continued labor income generation that supports near-term consumption and risk appetite, producing positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 5 trading days as an always-on momentum signal.
evidence_refs: fact:PAYEMS Sep-2017 vintage 146,659K vs revised 147,008K, indicating continued net job growth
failure_condition: Rank IC between monthly PAYEMS growth and 5-day forward SPX returns stays below 0.02 over any rolling 2-year window, or positive-PAYEMS-month hit rate is not significantly above the unconditional 65.5% base rate (p > 0.10).

mechanism_narrative: When FEDFUNDS rises while CPIAUCSL inflation growth simultaneously accelerates, real policy tightening intensifies faster than markets price in, raising discount rates disproportionately and producing negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days; the effect requires both conditions jointly, since rate hikes alone amid low inflation have historically been absorbed without equity stress.
candidate_trigger: FEDFUNDS up >0.25pp over 3 months AND CPIAUCSL YoY growth > 2%
evidence_refs: fact:CPIAUCSL Sep-2017 vintage 246.373 with FEDFUNDS at 1.15% amid gradual tightening cycle
regime_hint: monetary tightening cycle with rising inflation
failure_condition: In periods where both triggers are active, 20-day forward SPX returns are non-negative in more than 55% of instances, or the joint-trigger Rank IC does not exceed the single-series FEDFUNDS-only Rank IC by a statistically meaningful margin.

mechanism_narrative: A sustained decline in HOUST of more than 10% from its trailing 12-month peak signals weakening housing demand that historically leads broader consumption and construction-sector slowdown, producing negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 60 trading days.
evidence_refs: fact:HOUST Aug-2017 vintage 1,180K vs revised 1,159K, showing early softening from 2017 highs
failure_condition: Following the 10% HOUST decline trigger, 60-day forward SPX returns are positive in more than 55% of triggered instances, or Rank IC is statistically indistinguishable from zero (p > 0.10) across rolling 3-year windows.

mechanism_narrative: Joint deceleration in INDPRO growth combined with slowing PAYEMS growth signals a synchronized supply-and-labor-side cooling that markets underprice in the very short term, producing negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 5 trading days; neither indicator alone reliably predicts short-horizon returns.
candidate_trigger: INDPRO MoM growth < 0 AND PAYEMS MoM growth below trailing 6-month average
evidence_refs: fact:INDPRO and PAYEMS both showing revision-driven divergence from initial vintage values in 2017
failure_condition: The joint-trigger sample shows 5-day forward SPX return distribution not statistically different from unconditional distribution (KS test p > 0.10), or combined signal Rank IC does not exceed either single-series Rank IC over a 2-year rolling window.
